articles.headline: GSK pulls leucovorin from FDA review for autism
articles.summary: GSK withdrew its FDA application for Wellcovorin (leucovorin), a decades-old drug that Trump administration officials promoted as a potential autism treatment. The FDA had approved the drug only for a rare genetic condition affecting 46 patients, not autism broadly, after finding insufficient evidence for broader use.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights regulatory inconsistency and political pressure on FDA decision-making, with implications for evidence standards in drug approvals.

articles.headline: CMS proposes 2.4% hospital pay increase, nationwide mandatory model rollout
articles.summary: CMS proposed a 2.4% payment increase for hospitals in fiscal year 2027, alongside the first mandatory nationwide episode-based payment model for joint replacements (CJR-X). The model would hold hospitals accountable for costs during surgery and 90 days of recovery starting October 2027.
articles.why_it_matters: Represents significant shift toward value-based care with mandatory participation, affecting hospital margins and investment in orthopedic services.

articles.headline: AI assistance when contributing to the Linux kernel
articles.summary: Linux kernel maintainers published official guidelines for using AI coding assistants in kernel development. The documentation addresses legal, technical, and quality considerations for AI-generated code contributions to one of the world's most critical software projects.
articles.why_it_matters: Signals mainstream acceptance of AI tools in enterprise software development, with potential implications for productivity and code quality standards.
